mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects persistent consumer demand that translates into corporate revenue surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumption expectations, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate and CPI inflation captures the ex-ante real policy rate; when both series are elevated jointly, the restrictive real rate compresses valuation multiples and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are above their respective 12-month rolling 75th percentiles, the 20-day forward return is not negative on average (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, above-trend payroll employment growth signals persistent wage pressure and motivates additional federal funds rate increases, raising the equity discount rate and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening-cycle periods, positive month-over-month PAYEMS surprises coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contracting manufacturing sector that drags corporate earnings expectations lower, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Triggered consecutive-decline episodes fail to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect accelerating residential investment and construction employment, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are not followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates demand-pull expansion with confirmed supply response, reducing margin squeeze risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
